action.hypothesis: The routed soft-codebook family may now be leaving quality on the table because the router both selects supports and shrinks the decoded activation magnitudes. Decoupling those roles with a straight-through top-k path should keep router-driven support choice while decoding the full selected activations, which is most valuable to test first at K=96 because that point is already a stronger Pareto tradeoff than K=128 if quality holds.
action.summary: Run a full experiment at K=96 with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` after the code change in `sparsify/sparse_coder.py` that preserves router gradients but decodes unshrunk selected activations. Keep `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed so the only substantive change is the routed support-selection implementation on the more valuable high-quality/smaller-K anchor.
action.self_review: This is a single architectural hypothesis inside the current best family, not a coupled recipe sweep. I verified the edit with `py_compile` and a local forward pass. The main risk is that router-imposed magnitude shrinkage was acting as useful regularization, so removing it in the forward value could hurt despite the straight-through gradient path.
notes_to_memory: Introduced a straight-through routed top-k decode path for `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` so routing still controls support selection but selected activations are decoded at full encoder magnitude.
Chose K=96 as the first validation target because it is the more valuable high-quality Pareto point than K=128 if the code fix lowers FVU.
Launcher-level `GATED_TEMPERATURE` override is currently unusable in this execution path, so this round targets the routed-family cleanup directly in `sparsify/` instead of via env-only tuning.
next_hypotheses: If the straight-through routed decode lowers FVU at K=96, rerun the same fixed recipe at K=128 to see whether the gain also improves the top-quality anchor.
If it is neutral or worse at K=96, revert to the prior routed decode semantics and treat gate-based amplitude shrinkage as part of the family’s winning recipe.
If it wins clearly at K=96, consider propagating the same cleaned-up routed decode to the adjacent K=64 anchor before starting any new family exploration.

result.termination_reason: Behavioral diff: routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual encode() identical to topk — blocked
